steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The subject noun phrase is “New Mexico governor [Name].” On the right side of the blank is an appositive phrase “the first woman to serve in that role in the state's history” that restates the subject, followed by the main predicate “took office on January 1, 2011.” An em dash before the predicate marks where the appositive ends.

steps.title: Apply the em dash pairing rule
steps.content: Appositives set off by em dashes require matching em dashes on both sides. The blank needs an opening em dash to pair with the closing one after “history.” Comma, colon, and no punctuation all fail to properly set off the appositive. That matches choice D — D is correct.
